2605.29928.title: Label Over Logic? How Source Cues Bias Human Fallacy Judgments More Than LLMs
2605.29928.paper_summary: As AI-generated and AI-assisted content floods online spaces, source labels attached to such content can distort human reasoning judgments, with downstream consequences for moderation, evaluation, and decision-making. Whether LLMs share this vulnerability, or offer more source-agnostic evaluation, remains an open question with direct implications for human-AI collaboration. We examine this issue using logical fallacies as a controlled setting to isolate source-label effects on reasoning quality, independent of domain knowledge. We conduct an online study (N=505) where participants are assigned to a source condition (human, AI, human with AI assistance, AI with human assistance, or no disclosure) and evaluate comments containing logical fallacies, comparing their judgments with those of LLMs (GPT-5.2, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.5), who were evaluated across the same source conditions. Human evaluators were significantly more susceptible to fallacies labeled as written by human or human with AI assistance and assigned higher trust and evaluation ratings in these conditions. LLM evaluations remained comparatively stable across source labels, though performance varied across models. Confidence levels were similarly high across conditions for both humans and LLMs, regardless of fallacy presence. Our findings indicate that source-label bias in reasoning evaluation is primarily a human vulnerability and highlight the potential of human-LLM collaboration in increasingly AI-mediated environments.
2605.29928.paper_summary_zh: 隨著AI生成和AI輔助的內容充斥在線空間，附加於這些內容的來源標籤可能會扭曲人類的推理判斷，並對審核、評估和決策產生下游影響。LLMs是否也存在這種脆弱性，或提供更具來源無關性的評估，仍然是一個未解的問題，對人類與AI的合作具有直接的影響。我們使用邏輯謬誤作為控制環境來檢視這個問題，以孤立來源標籤對推理質量的影響，與領域知識無關。我們進行了一項在線研究（N=505），參與者被分配到一個來源條件（人類、AI、人類與AI輔助、AI與人類輔助或不披露）並評估包含邏輯謬誤的評論，將他們的判斷與LLMs（GPT-5.2、Gemini 2.5 Flash、Claude Sonnet 4.5）的判斷進行比較，這些LLMs在相同的來源條件下進行評估。人類評估者對標記為人類或人類與AI輔助所寫的謬誤顯著更容易受到影響，並在這些條件下給予更高的信任和評估評分。LLM的評估在來源標籤之間保持相對穩定，儘管不同模型的表現有所變化。無論謬誤的存在與否，人類和LLMs在各條件下的信心水平都同樣高。我們的研究結果表明，推理評估中的來源標籤偏見主要是人類的脆弱性，並突顯了在日益AI介導的環境中人類與LLM合作的潛力。
2605.29928.author: Mahjabin Nahar et.al.
2605.29928.authors: Mahjabin Nahar, Nafis Irtiza Tripto, Aiping Xiong, Ting-Hao `Kenneth' Huang, Dongwon Lee
